purpose: SEP-derived, operator-bound, machine-readable implementation checklist for MCP stateless/sessionless migration. Update this file when official SEP status or repo implementation status changes.

operator_binding.source_context: operator D1-D12 answers based on official MCP SEP index; chat attachment Wklejony tekst.txt 2026-06-25
operator_binding.summary: D3-D6/D9 direct the workbench toward Sessionless MCP, explicit state handles, no DELETE /mcp teardown, per-request streams/cancellation, and event-driven list lifecycle. Operator correction 2026-06-30 further clarifies that SSE and permanent dual-route coexistence are migration debt, not the target architecture. D7 directs Resource/Scope Matrix Enforcement as separate approved work.

tracked_final_seps.title: Sessionless MCP via Explicit State Handles
Specification Feature Lifecycle and Deprecation Policy

source_verification.coverage_rule: Every Final SEP from the official index must appear in all_seps_index; core sessionless/stateless SEPs also require deprecation_ledger entries.

deprecation_ledger.migration_path: server/discover plus per-request protocol version and client capability metadata

checklist.item: map current initialize handlers
design parallel stateless mode before removal
prepare historical workbench deprecation/standardization boundary for the hidden prototype route
bridge server/discover and per-request metadata onto stable /mcp without removing legacy initialize
record final legacy boundary for initialize on surviving /mcp
retire initialize-created transport sessions on surviving /mcp while keeping legacy initialize as stateless compatibility

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: stable /mcp no longer depends on MCP-Session-Id; session header handling survives only as residual historical debt outside the active surviving-route contract
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: explicit state handles returned by tools and passed as ordinary arguments

checklist.item: map session header sites
prevent new strategic dependency on transport sessions
keep the current stable session route separate from the historical hidden prototype route
prepare historical workbench deprecation/standardization boundary for the hidden prototype route
retire stable /mcp transport-session header dependence

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: stable /mcp no longer creates or requires transport sessions; SessionStore is removed from the active repo, while remaining McpSession/outbound helpers still exist as bounded cleanup debt
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: parallel sessionless target with request-scoped or explicit state handles

checklist.item: map SessionStore responsibilities
record the historical target-selection decision for the parallel prototype track
prepare the historical target-selection readiness packet for the parallel prototype track
implement the historical hidden sessionless prototype
prepare historical workbench deprecation/standardization boundary for the hidden prototype route
retire active surviving-route transport-session lifecycle
remove residual SessionStore and push/SSE helper files that no longer serve active /mcp

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: stable GET /mcp now returns 405 and the residual GET-SSE helper file is removed from the active repo
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: subscriptions/listen RPC over POST in stateless target

checklist.item: map GET SSE stream and tests
design subscriptions/listen compatibility matrix
prepare historical workbench deprecation/standardization boundary for the hidden prototype route
apply bounded GET teardown on surviving /mcp route
remove residual unreachable GET-SSE helper files from the active repo

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: removed from active runtime; historical isolated validation previously exercised request-scoped SSE on the hidden sessionless prototype route, but current stable runtime still does not implement it
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: for current TEST MCP tool-surface freshness, do not preserve subscriptions/listen on surviving /mcp; use pull-only tools/list freshness with ttlMs=0, cacheScope=private, and current tool-surface metadata

checklist.item: record current official name subscriptions/listen
record compatibility matrix against stable GET /mcp SSE and list_changed dry-run
test first on isolated port
record stricter no-SSE project contract boundary
record surviving-route replacement scope before prototype-route retirement
record final pull-only no-SSE contract for tool-surface freshness on surviving /mcp
retire prototype-only subscriptions/listen runtime path and stop advertising tool-list push on active /mcp

deprecation_ledger.migration_path: do not implement DELETE teardown; remove protocol sessions instead

checklist.item: record operator D4

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: Request abort propagation, client-disconnect write guards, and cooperative tool cancellation are implemented for the stable-compatible runtime; JSON-RPC notifications/cancelled and draft transport semantics remain target-selection dependent
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: HTTP stream close cancels that request; timeout remains fallback

checklist.item: operator D6 recorded
implement request cancellation context plumbing
implement disconnect and cooperative cancellation guards

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: stable /mcp replay behavior is no longer reachable and residual replay helper files are removed from the active repo
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: connection drop cancels request; durable work uses tasks primitive

checklist.item: map replay tests
avoid new dependency on resumable SSE
remove stable-route replay reachability
remove residual replay helper files from the active repo

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: tools/list explicitly returns ttlMs=0 and cacheScope=private; no active top-level resources/prompts handlers exist; tools/call list/read-like payloads do not yet share a generic freshness layer
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: add ttlMs and cacheScope; list_changed remains immediate invalidation

checklist.item: record TTL/cacheScope target
inventory all list/read result builders

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: HPL1-HPL4 reconciled; HPL5 runtime apply prototype remains gated pending explicit operator runtime step
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: event-driven registry plus list_changed and TTL/cacheScope fallback

checklist.item: operator D9 recorded
create hotplug registry design

deprecation_ledger.migration_path: do not expand deprecated core features as strategic dependencies

checklist.item: inventory sampling usage
inventory roots and protocol logging usage

deprecation_ledger.migration_path: each protocol-direction item tracks SEP source, lifecycle state, migration path, status, and evidence

checklist.item: add lifecycle/status schema
add smoke guard

deprecation_ledger.component: runtime restart resilience and process topology boundary
deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: SERVER_RUNTIME_TOPOLOGY_SPEC.json defines active topology; 3008 OAuth21 is supervisor-managed and live-loaded after Stage 14.5; public 3009 is not currently live locally; 3009 false closeout corrected and must not recur.
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: use supervisor-controlled 3008 restart only when a workflow step and operator intent authorize it; stateless direction reduces dependence on session/process lifetime

checklist.item: record false closeout correction
recover or recreate file-triggered restart authority
prove OAuth21 3008 restart preserves startup arguments
create machine-readable runtime topology/restart authority boundary

deprecation_ledger.repo_current_model: Stage14.5 gate committed and OAuth21 3008 live-loaded after later supervisor-managed restart; no current restart-authority blocker remains for 3008.
deprecation_ledger.migration_path: continue as separately approved security work with negative controls and audit receipts

checklist.item: Stage14.5 repo apply committed
OAuth21 3008 runtime restart/load validation

recommended_next: Current SSE-based and dual-route behavior must be treated as migration debt, not as the target architecture.
SEP-2549 inventory is complete: tools/list already exposes ttlMs=0 and cacheScope=private, while broader tool-level freshness remains undecided.

Stable /mcp transport-session retirement is complete: initialize no longer creates transport state, session headers are ignored on stable POST, and server/discover now reports protocol_sessions=false.
Residual GET-SSE/SessionStore/list-changed repo cleanup is complete: unreachable helper files were removed without changing the surviving /mcp contract.
Prototype-route retirement scoping is complete and now remains historical evidence only because hidden /mcp/sessionless is already retired from active repo/runtime truth.
Initialize-retirement boundary is complete: legacy initialize on surviving /mcp is compatibility-only and must not grow as target-facing protocol surface.

Replacement-coverage scoping is complete and now remains historical evidence; the remaining bounded cleanup is residual unreachable session/SSE debt on surviving /mcp rather than hidden-route retirement authorization.

Final subscriptions/listen decision is complete: TEST MCP will not preserve tool-surface freshness push on surviving /mcp and will use pull-only tools/list freshness with ttlMs=0 and cacheScope=private.
Pull-only runtime package is complete: stable /mcp no longer advertises listChanged, and hidden /mcp/sessionless no longer implements subscriptions/listen.
State-handle fate decision is complete: explicit state handles remain a tool-design pattern, but route-level state/handle methods do not survive on the final /mcp surface.
Hidden-route retirement package is complete in repo: active runtime code no longer wires /mcp/sessionless and no longer exposes MCP_TEST_ENABLE_SESSIONLESS_PROTOTYPE as active runtime config.
Hidden-route retirement is now live-verified on OAuth21 3008: hidden /mcp/sessionless returns 404 after the controlled restart while stable /mcp still reports 43 tools.
Historical /mcp/sessionless live-operation artifacts are quarantined: they now remain traceability evidence only and must not be treated as the active target architecture.
Treat the no-SSE destination as a stricter project target than the current generic MCP Streamable HTTP draft; do not misstate request-scoped SSE as forbidden by MCP itself.
For every next-step recommendation, reassess blockers, connector refresh, and whether OAuth21 3008 restart is required; assistant may restart 3008 when workflow and operator intent authorize it.
Adjacent runtime-contract SEP triage is complete: existing runtime/spec/test coverage, explicit non-targets, and a bounded partial-coverage watchlist are now recorded.
Auth/security adjacent SEP triage is complete: existing auth coverage, explicit non-targets, and the remaining watchlist are now recorded.
Adjacent/auth watchlist review is complete: no new dedicated ledger is opened now; reopen only when a tracked trigger surface becomes active target work.
The first semantics-sensitive watch item is SEP-1303 if tool-validation-error behavior becomes an active migration topic.
Repo cleanup/normalization debt is closed on main: cleanup anchor aecec58 remains merged in main history, and later workflow-only commits do not reopen this debt.
Current local-only untracked directories `.codebase-memory/` and `_workflow/experiments/` remain intentionally outside committed scope and do not reopen the cleanup blocker.
Treat this file as a living SEP-driven checklist.
